ag.raw_text: required if more than 1000 residents notified; same timing as individuals (within 45 days).

penalty.raw_text: up to $5000 per day for delay, maximum $500000 per breach; enforced by attorney general as unlawful trade practice; no private right of action.

ag.raw_text: required if the entity decides not to notify individuals because it determined the breach is unlikely to cause harm.

penalty.raw_text: civil penalty up to $500 per resident not notified, capped at $50000 per incident. violations may also be treated as unfair or deceptive acts under alaska law with potential additional remedies.

ag.raw_text: within 45 days after the determination that more than 1000 individuals must be notifiyed, along with the the arizona dept of homeland security head and the 3 largest national consumer reporting agencies.

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general may impose a civil penalty for a violation of this article not to exceed the lesser of $10000 per affected individual or the total amount of economic loss sustained by affected individuals, but the maximum civil penalty from a breach or series of related breaches may not exceed $500000.

ag.raw_text: must notify ag if more than 1000 residents affected, at the same time as notice to consumers or within 45 days of determining likelihood of harm

penalty.raw_text: ag may bring action under deceptive trade practices act; civil penalties, private remedies possible

ag.raw_text: required if more than 500 residents must be notified. a sample of the consumer notice must be submitted to the attorney general.

penalty.raw_text: individuals may bring a civil action if they are injured. the attorney general may also take action for violations.

ag.raw_text: notify colorado attorney general within 30 days if 500+ residents affected.

penalty.raw_text: civil penalties possible.

ag.raw_text: must be provided "not later than the time when notice is provided to the resident." the ag has an online portal for this.

penalty.raw_text: a violation of the breach notification law is considered an **unfair trade practice** and is enforceable by the attorney general under the connecticut unfair trade practices act (cutpa).

ag.raw_text: must notify the attorney general if residents are notified

penalty.raw_text: attorney general can seek enforcement; penalties may be imposed for non-compliance

ag.raw_text: required if breach affects more than 500 residents; written notice by 30 days including synopsis, number affected, services offered, contact info, and copy of notice to individuals.

penalty.raw_text: civil penalties up to $500000 for violations; attorney general enforcement; potential unfair/deceptive trade practice liability.

ag.raw_text: no explicit requirement for ag notice; but other regulatory notice may apply for specific regulated entities.

penalty.raw_text: unfair/deceptive practice enforceable by the attorney general; no private right of action; penalties as determined under ga fair business practices act.

ag.raw_text: if a business notifies more than 1000 persons at one time, it must, without unreasonable delay, notify the state of hawaii office of consumer protection of the timing, distribution, and content of the consumer notice.

penalty.raw_text: businesses may face civil penalties up to 2500 dollars per violation and are liable for actual damages to injured persons. actions may be brought by the attorney general or office of consumer protection. injured individuals can also bring civil actions and may recover attorneys’ fees. penalties are in addition to other remedies under hawaii law.

ag.raw_text: public agencies must notify ag within 24 hours of discovery. no ag notice required for private entities.

penalty.raw_text: civil penalties available.

ag.raw_text: notice shall be made in the most expedient time possible and without unreasonable delay.

penalty.raw_text: any person, including but not limited to a third party referenced in subsection (c), who violates this section is subject to a civil penalty of not more than $100 for each individual with respect to whom personal information is disposed of in violation of this section. a civil penalty may not, however, exceed $50000 for each instance of improper disposal of materials containing personal information. the attorney general may impose a civil penalty after notice to the person accused of violating this section and an opportunity for that person to be heard in the matter. the attorney general may file a civil action in the circuit court to recover any penalty imposed under this section. (e) in addition to the authority to impose a civil penalty under subsection (d), the attorney general may bring an action in the circuit court to remedy a violation of this section, seeking any appropriate relief. (f) a financial institution under 15 u.s.c. 6801 et. seq. or any person subject to 15 u.s.c. 1681w is exempt from this section.

ag.raw_text: notice shall be made without unreasonable delay.

penalty.raw_text: not specified

ag.raw_text: notice shall be made in the most expeditious manner possible and without unreasonable delay.

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general shall enforce this section

ag.raw_text: notice shall be made in the most expeditious manner possible and without unreasonable delay.

penalty.raw_text: for violations of this section, except as to insurance companies licensed to do business in this state, the attorney general is empowered to bring an action in law or equity to address violations of this section and for other relief that may be appropriate.

ag.raw_text: not specified

penalty.raw_text: not specified

ag.raw_text: notice shall be made in the most expeditious manner possible and without unreasonable delay.

penalty.raw_text: a violation is treated as an **unfair or deceptive act or practice** under mass. gen. laws ch. 93a. the attorney general may bring an action to seek injunctive relief and civil penalties.

ag.raw_text: notice must be made without unreasonable delay after discovery of the breach.

penalty.raw_text: (h) for violations of this section by an insurance company licensed to do business in this state, the insurance commissioner shall have the sole authority to enforce the provisions of this section.

ag.raw_text: not specified

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general shall enforce this section

ag.raw_text: only required if insurance-specific or >100 residdednts with senate bill 2046

penalty.raw_text: failure to comply constitutes an unfair trade practice, enforceable by the attorney general; no private right of action specified; possible civil penalties.

ag.raw_text: required if more than 1000 residents notified

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general shall have exclusive authority to bring an action to obtain actual damages for a willful and knowing violation of this section and may seek a civil penalty not to exceed one hundred fifty thousand dollars per breach of the security of the system or series of breaches of a similar nature that are discovered in a single investigation.

ag.raw_text: submit an electronic copy of the notification and a statement providing the date and method of distribution of the notification to the attorney general's consumer protection office, excluding any information that personally identifies any individual who is entitled to receive notification.

penalty.raw_text: (2) in an action brought under 30-14-111, if the court finds that a person is willfully using or has willfully used a method, act, or practice declared unlawful by 30-14-103, the department, upon petition to the court, may recover on behalf of the state a civil fine of not more than $10000 for each violation. the fine provided for in this subsection is in addition to any liability that a person might be subject to under subsection (1). (3) a person who engages in a fraudulent course of conduct declared unlawful by 30-14-103 shall upon conviction be fined an amount not more than $5000, imprisoned for not more than 1 year, or both, in the discretion of the court. this subsection does not limit any other provision of this part. (4) for purposes of this section, a willful violation occurs when the party committing the violation knew or should have known that the conduct was a violation of 30-14-103.

ag.raw_text: required: not later than the time notice is provided to the nebraska resident.

penalty.raw_text: enforced by the ag; violation is an unlawful act or practice under the consumer protection act.

ag.raw_text: not explicitly required by statute. ag suggests voluntary notice via email.

penalty.raw_text: civil penalty not to exceed $5000 for each violation

ag.raw_text: the notice shall include the anticipated date of the notice to the individuals and the approximate number of individuals in this state who will be notified. nothing in this section shall be construed to require the person to provide to any regulator or the new hampshire attorney general's office the names of the individuals entitled to receive the notice or any personal information relating to them. the disclosure shall be made to affected individuals as quickly as possible, after the determination required under this section
required, must report breach to ag in advance of consumers

penalty.raw_text: penalty of not more than $10000 for the first offense and not more than $20000 for the second and each subsequent offense

ag.raw_text: mandatory
if the incident affects over five hundred residents of new york, the person or business shall provide the written determination to the state attorney general within ten days after the determination.

penalty.raw_text: failure to notify: up to **$20 per instance** (max $250000). failure to maintain safeguards: up to $5000 per violation.

ag.raw_text: required if notice is provided to affected consumers

penalty.raw_text: violation of g.s. 75-1.1

ag.raw_text: disclose to ag if breach exceeds 250 individuals

penalty.raw_text: civil penalties up to $5000

ag.raw_text: not required, but must report to consumer reporting agency if more than 1000 residents affected
not specified
required if the breach affects more than 250 oregon residents. a copy of the consumer notice must be provided.

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general may bring an action for civil penalties. penalties may reach up to 1000 dollars per violation, with overall limits determined by oregon consumer protection law.

ag.raw_text: if more than 1000 pennsylvania residents are notified, must also notify nationwide consumer reporting agencies

penalty.raw_text: attorney general may enforce; civil penalties possible; direct cause of action for damages

ag.raw_text: required if the breach affects **more than 500** rhode island residents. notification to the ag must be made "without delaying notice to affected rhode island residents."

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general may bring action. • **reckless violation:** civil penalty of not more than \*\*$100 per record\*\*. • **knowing and willful violation:** civil penalty of not more than **$200 per record**.

ag.raw_text: if >1000 residents notified, must notify department of consumer affairs’ consumer protection division without unreasonable delay, providing notice details.

penalty.raw_text: administrative fine up to $1000 per resident per violation; private right of action and ag enforcement; injunctive relief and attorney fees possible.

ag.raw_text: notify south dakota ag if >250 residents affected.

penalty.raw_text: civil penalties possible.

ag.raw_text: no general statewide ag notification requirement for private sector breaches; state agencies must notify the comptroller within 5 working days

penalty.raw_text: civil lawsuits may be brought for failure to notify within required 45 days; ag enforcement and damages possible

ag.raw_text: required if more than 250 resiedents

penalty.raw_text: (a) a person who violates this chapter is liable to this state for a civil penalty of at least $2000 but not more than $50000 for each violation. the attorney general may bring an action to recover the civil penalty imposed under this subsection. (a-1) in addition to penalties assessed under subsection (a), a person who fails to take reasonable action to comply with section 521.053(b) is liable to this state for a civil penalty of not more than $100 for each individual to whom notification is due under that subsection for each consecutive day that the person fails to take reasonable action to comply with that subsection. civil penalties under this section may not exceed $250000 for all individuals to whom notification is due after a single breach. the attorney general may bring an action to recover the civil penalties imposed under this subsection.

ag.raw_text: notify utah attorney general and utah cyber center if 500+ residents affected.

penalty.raw_text: civil penalties possible.

ag.raw_text: a **preliminary notice** must be sent to the ag within **14 business days** of discovery (one of the fastest in the nation). a full notice must be sent at the same time as notice to individuals.

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general or a state's attorney may bring an action. • **government agencies:** civil penalty of $500 per resident not notified, capped at $50000. • **private actions:** a consumer may sue for "actual economic damages" sustained. • **ag enforcement:** violations are treated as an unfair or deceptive act in commerce.

ag.raw_text: required if more than 1000 residents

penalty.raw_text: violations may result in damages, civil penalties, including up to $5000 per violation per day for ag notification delay, and private right of action. unfair trade practices provisions may apply.

ag.raw_text: required if residents are notified

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general or the pfr may bring an action. penalties include: • equitable relief; • fines of **$500 per violation**, capped at **$2500 for each day** the entity is in violation.

ag.raw_text: attorney general must be notified prior to notice to individuals if any maryland resident is notified

penalty.raw_text: attorney general may bring enforcement actions; civil penalties possible per violation

ag.raw_text: attorney general (ag) notification is required for any breach affecting resident(s) of the state, without unreasonable delay

penalty.raw_text: attorney general may impose a civil penalty not to exceed $150000 per breach; individuals may seek direct economic damages in civil action

ag.raw_text: required if the breach affects 500 or more washington residents. notice must be given to the attorney general within 30 days and must include detailed information about the incident.

penalty.raw_text: the attorney general may bring actions for violations. civil penalties may apply under washington consumer protection laws, and courts may order restitution.

ag.raw_text: no general requirement to notify attorney general (ag) for private sector entities; ag notification may be required for violations or enforcement action

penalty.raw_text: civil penalties up to $150000 per breach or series of similar breaches may be imposed by the attorney general; failure to comply constitutes unfair/deceptive practices

ag.raw_text: not specified in statute.

penalty.raw_text: whoever is concerned in the commission of a violation of this chapter for which a forfeiture is imposed is a principal and may be charged with and convicted of the violation although he or she did not directly commit it and although the person who directly committed it has not been convicted of the violation.

ag.raw_text: no ag/government notification requirement.

penalty.raw_text: civil penalties under consumer protection.
